---
title: "Dynamic Replacement Model: Fit Summary across 4p, 8p, and 8p+FE Specifications"
author: "Kaleb Javier"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
format:
  pdf:
    documentclass: article
    geometry: margin=1in
    fontsize: 11pt
    linestretch: 1.15
    toc: true
    number-sections: true
    keep-tex: true
    fig-cap-location: top
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        \usepackage{amsmath}
        \usepackage{amssymb}
        \usepackage{booktabs}
        \usepackage{float}
        \usepackage{longtable}
        \usepackage{graphicx}
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo    = FALSE,
  warning = FALSE,
  message = FALSE,
  fig.pos = "H"
)
library(data.table)
library(knitr)
library(kableExtra)
library(here)
```

# Introduction {#sec-intro}

This report summarizes the in-sample fit of the dynamic structural replacement
model under four specifications, each estimated by Aguirregabiria-Mira NPL on
the observed UST panel ($N = 2{,}282{,}735$ tank-year observations):

1. **4-parameter** — a single $(\kappa, K, \gamma_{\text{price}}, \gamma_{\text{risk}})$
   block, pooled across wall and regime.
2. **8-parameter** — the same primitives split by wall type (SW/DW) and
   insurance regime (FF/RB): 4 cost parameters and 4 price/risk slopes.
3. **8-parameter + FE (M-only, joint)** — the 8p structural specification
   augmented with 17 state-level intercepts $\alpha_g$ entering the
   *Maintain* flow utility only. Estimated jointly (25 parameters in `optim`).
4. **8-parameter + stayFE (profile)** — the same 8 structural parameters with
   17 state-level intercepts entering both the *Maintain* and *Replace* flow
   utilities (the "stay-in-business" actions), with the $\alpha_g$
   *profiled out* via a 1-D Newton FOC inside each likelihood evaluation
   (so `optim` searches only the 8 structural dimensions). The exit
   utility is unchanged across all four specifications and serves as the
   outside option.

All four specifications use the *Semantic 2* convention for the
$\alpha_g$: the FE enter the measurement equation (the likelihood) only,
not the agent's value function in counterfactual re-solves. Texas is the
baseline ($\alpha_{\text{TX}} \equiv 0$).

The report is organized for direct visual and statistical comparison:
@sec-models gives the flow-utility specifications;
@sec-params reports the four parameter tables and the FE alphas;
@sec-comparison reports the four-way information-criteria and
likelihood-ratio table plus an aggregate fit-metric comparison;
@sec-fit reports model-implied vs. empirical action shares at the
$(\text{wall} \times \text{regime} \times \text{age bin})$ cell level for each
specification, with figures produced by a single shared plotting routine to
guarantee structural parity. Per-cell numerical fit tables are in @sec-appendix.

Artifacts are produced by `Code/Dynamic_Model/04k_Fit_Report_Artifacts.R`
(specs 1–3) and `Code/Dynamic_Model/04l_8paramFE_Profile_Estimation.R`
(spec 4) from saved fit objects under `Output/Estimation_Results/`. No
estimation is re-run in the production of this report.


# Model Specifications {#sec-models}

All four specifications share the same dynamic discrete-choice (DCC)
structure with three actions $a_t \in \{M, E, R\}$ (Maintain, Exit, Replace),
discount factor $\beta = 0.95$, T1EV(0) shocks with scale $\sigma_2 = 1$,
and a state space of $S = 32$ cells indexed by tank age bin
$A_{\text{bin}} \in \{1, \ldots, 8\}$ (5-year bins), wall type
$w \in \{\text{SW}, \text{DW}\}$, and insurance regime
$\rho \in \{\text{FF}, \text{RB}\}$. The exit action is absorbing
(`F_exit` does not enter the Bellman operator), so $u_E$ is the firm's
terminal outside-option payoff. All cost parameters $\kappa$ and $K$ are
reported in dollar units (one model unit $= \$10{,}000 / \text{yr} /
\text{facility}$).

## 4-parameter flow utilities {#sec-models-4p}

A single $(\kappa, K, \gamma_p, \gamma_r)$ vector applies to every state
cell. The flow utilities at state $s$ are:

$$
\begin{aligned}
u_M(s; \theta_{4p}) &\;=\; 1 \,+\, \gamma_{\text{price}} \cdot P(s) \,-\, \gamma_{\text{risk}} \cdot h(s) \\
u_E(s; \theta_{4p}) &\;=\; \kappa \\
u_R(s; \theta_{4p}) &\;=\; -K \;=\; -\exp(K_{\log})
\end{aligned}
$$

where $P(s)$ is the per-period insurance premium in cell $s$, $h(s)$ is
the cell's hazard-loss product (probability of release $\times$ expected
cleanup cost), and the leading $1$ in $u_M$ is the normalized annual net
revenue $R = \$10{,}000/\text{yr}$ collected each period the facility
operates. Parameter vector:
$\theta_{4p} = (\kappa_{\text{exit}}, K_{\log}, \gamma_{\text{price}}, \gamma_{\text{risk}})$,
4 entries.

## 8-parameter flow utilities {#sec-models-8p}

Cost parameters split by wall, price/risk semi-elasticities split by
regime:

$$
\begin{aligned}
u_M(s; \theta_{8p}) &\;=\; 1 \,+\, \gamma_{\text{price}, \rho(s)} \cdot P(s) \,-\, \gamma_{\text{risk}, \rho(s)} \cdot h(s) \\
u_E(s; \theta_{8p}) &\;=\; \kappa_{w(s)} \\
u_R(s; \theta_{8p}) &\;=\; -K_{w(s)} \;=\; -\exp(K_{\log, w(s)})
\end{aligned}
$$

with $w(s) \in \{\text{SW}, \text{DW}\}$ and
$\rho(s) \in \{\text{FF}, \text{RB}\}$ read off the state cell. Parameter
vector:
$\theta_{8p} = (\kappa_{\text{SW}}, \kappa_{\text{DW}}, K_{\log, \text{SW}},
K_{\log, \text{DW}}, \gamma_{\text{price}, \text{FF}}, \gamma_{\text{price}, \text{RB}},
\gamma_{\text{risk}, \text{FF}}, \gamma_{\text{risk}, \text{RB}})$, 8 entries.

Sign conventions: $\gamma_{\text{price}} < 0$ in the parameter (premium
*reduces* maintain utility); $\gamma_{\text{risk}} > 0$ in the parameter
but enters with a minus sign in the equation, so higher hazard also
*reduces* maintain utility.

## 8-parameter + FE (M-only, joint) {#sec-models-8pfe}

Adds an additive group intercept $\alpha_g$ to the Maintain flow only,
indexed by the firm's state of operation $g \in \{0 = \text{TX}, 1 = \text{AR}, \ldots, 17 = \text{VA}\}$:

$$
\begin{aligned}
u_M(s, g; \theta_{8p}, \alpha_g) &\;=\; v_M(s; \theta_{8p}) \,+\, \alpha_g \\
u_E(s; \theta_{8p}) &\;=\; v_E(s; \theta_{8p}) \\
u_R(s; \theta_{8p}) &\;=\; v_R(s; \theta_{8p})
\end{aligned}
$$

where $v_j(s; \theta)$ are the NPL choice-specific values from the
existing 8p model (i.e., the same flow utilities as @sec-models-8p
plus continuation values where applicable). Texas is the baseline
($\alpha_0 \equiv 0$). The 17 control-state alphas are estimated
*jointly* with $\theta_{8p}$ in a 25-parameter `optim` call. Parameter
vector: $\theta_{8p}$ plus
$(\alpha_{\text{AR}}, \alpha_{\text{CO}}, \ldots, \alpha_{\text{VA}})$,
$8 + 17 = 25$ entries.

## 8-parameter + stayFE (profile) {#sec-models-8pfeStay}

The state intercept $\alpha_g$ shifts both *Maintain* and *Replace*
utilities — the two "stay-in-business" actions — leaving the exit
(outside-option) utility untouched:

$$
\begin{aligned}
u_M(s, g; \theta_{8p}, \alpha_g) &\;=\; v_M(s; \theta_{8p}) \,+\, \alpha_g \\
u_E(s; \theta_{8p}) &\;=\; v_E(s; \theta_{8p}) \\
u_R(s, g; \theta_{8p}, \alpha_g) &\;=\; v_R(s; \theta_{8p}) \,+\, \alpha_g
\end{aligned}
$$

Crucially, $\alpha_g$ is *profiled out* of the likelihood: for each
$\theta_{8p}$ candidate the optimizer evaluates, the 17 control-state
$\alpha_g$ values are solved analytically from a 1-D Newton FOC enforcing

$$
\sum_s \left[\, n_M(s, g) + n_R(s, g) \,-\, n_{\text{total}}(s, g) \cdot \hat P_{\text{stay}}(s, g; \theta_{8p}, \alpha_g) \,\right] \;=\; 0
$$

(i.e., the model-implied total "stay" count in group $g$ equals the
empirical count). `optim` therefore searches only the 8 structural
dimensions. Texas is the baseline ($\alpha_0 \equiv 0$). At
convergence, the $\alpha_g(\hat\theta)$ values are recovered post-hoc.
See `Reports/Teaching_Notes_Dynamic_Models.md` §1 for the full
derivation, literature, and standard-error treatment. Parameter vector:
8 structural plus 17 profiled-out alphas; effective $k = 25$ for
information-criteria purposes.


# Parameter Estimates {#sec-params}

## 4-parameter specification {#sec-params-4p}

@tbl-theta-4p reports point estimates with AM (2002) profile-likelihood
standard errors and 95% Wald confidence intervals.

```{r tbl-theta-4p, results="asis"}
#| label: tbl-theta-4p
#| tbl-cap: "4-parameter NPL estimates: pooled $\\kappa$, $K$, $\\gamma_{\\text{price}}$, $\\gamma_{\\text{risk}}$."
cat("\\begin{center}\n\\resizebox{\\textwidth}{!}{\n")
cat(readLines(here("Output", "Tables", "04g_Theta_Table_AM_with_CIs.tex")),
    sep = "\n")
cat("\n}\n\\end{center}\n")
```

## 8-parameter specification {#sec-params-8p}

@tbl-theta-8p splits $\kappa$ and $K$ by wall type (SW/DW) and the price/risk
slopes by insurance regime (FF/RB).

```{r tbl-theta-8p, results="asis"}
#| label: tbl-theta-8p
#| tbl-cap: "8-parameter NPL estimates: $\\kappa$ and $K$ split by wall (SW/DW); $\\gamma_{\\text{price}}$ and $\\gamma_{\\text{risk}}$ split by regime (FF/RB)."
cat("\\begin{center}\n\\resizebox{\\textwidth}{!}{\n")
cat(readLines(here("Output", "Tables", "04h_Theta_Table_8param_AM_SE.tex")),
    sep = "\n")
cat("\n}\n\\end{center}\n")
```

## 8-parameter + FE (M-only, joint) {#sec-params-8pfe}

@tbl-theta-8pfe reports the 8-structural-parameter block from the joint
25-dim $(\theta, \alpha)$ estimation with $\alpha_g$ entering $u_M$
only. The 17 state fixed effects are reported separately in
@tbl-fe-alphas.

```{r tbl-theta-8pfe, results="asis"}
#| label: tbl-theta-8pfe
#| tbl-cap: "8-parameter + FE NPL estimates: structural parameters from the joint $(\\theta, \\alpha)$ fit. The 17 state fixed effects $\\alpha_g$ are reported separately in @tbl-fe-alphas (omitted here to avoid duplication)."
lns <- readLines(here("Output", "Tables", "04i_Theta_Table_8paramFE_AM_SE.tex"))
lns <- lns[!grepl("\\\\alpha_", lns)]
cat("\\begin{center}\n\\resizebox{\\textwidth}{!}{\n")
cat(lns, sep = "\n")
cat("\n}\n\\end{center}\n")
```

### State fixed-effect coefficients {#sec-params-fe-alphas}

The state fixed effects $\alpha_g$ enter the measurement equation (the
likelihood) only — they shift the model-implied probability of *Maintain*
in state $g$ relative to the TX baseline. They do **not** enter the agent's
equilibrium value function during counterfactual re-solves, per the
"Semantic 2" convention adopted in this codebase.

```{r tbl-fe-alphas, results="asis"}
#| label: tbl-fe-alphas
#| tbl-cap: "State fixed-effect coefficients $\\hat\\alpha_g$ from the 8p+FE specification. Texas is the baseline ($\\alpha_{\\text{TX}} \\equiv 0$); standard errors in parentheses."
cat("\\begin{center}\n")
cat(readLines(here("Output", "Tables", "04k_FE_Alphas_Compact.tex")),
    sep = "\n")
cat("\n\\end{center}\n")
```

*Note on extreme estimates.* The Missouri ($\hat\alpha_{\text{MO}} = 8.18$,
SE $0.77$) and South Dakota ($\hat\alpha_{\text{SD}} = 2.27$, SE $0.08$)
coefficients are substantially larger than the other 15 control-state alphas,
which span roughly $[-1.4,\, +0.8]$. Both are statistically distinguishable
from zero and reflect near-zero observed *Exit* and *Replace* activity in those
state-year cells relative to the TX baseline; they are not numerical artifacts.

## 8-parameter + stayFE (profile) {#sec-params-8pfeStay}

@tbl-theta-8pfeProfile reports the 8 structural parameters from the
profile-likelihood fit. The 17 state intercepts $\alpha_g$ are recovered
post-hoc at $\hat\theta$ and reported in @tbl-fe-alphas-profile.

```{r tbl-theta-8pfeProfile, results="asis"}
#| label: tbl-theta-8pfeProfile
#| tbl-cap: "8-parameter + stayFE (profile) estimates: structural parameters only. The 17 state FE coefficients $\\alpha_g$ (profiled out at $\\hat\\theta$) are reported separately in @tbl-fe-alphas-profile."
cat("\\begin{center}\n\\resizebox{\\textwidth}{!}{\n")
cat(readLines(here("Output", "Tables", "04l_Theta_Table_8paramFE_profile_AM_SE.tex")),
    sep = "\n")
cat("\n}\n\\end{center}\n")
```

### State fixed-effect coefficients (profile spec) {#sec-params-fe-alphas-profile}

```{r tbl-fe-alphas-profile, results="asis"}
#| label: tbl-fe-alphas-profile
#| tbl-cap: "State fixed-effect coefficients $\\hat\\alpha_g(\\hat\\theta)$ from the stayFE-profile specification, recovered post-convergence from the profile FOC. Texas is the baseline ($\\alpha_{\\text{TX}} \\equiv 0$); standard errors in parentheses are from the joint 25-dim Hessian sub-block. Several SE values report as 0.000 due to finite-difference precision in `numDeriv::hessian`; these are not exact zeros but values below the 3-decimal display threshold."
cat("\\begin{center}\n")
cat(readLines(here("Output", "Tables", "04l_FE_Alphas_profile_Compact.tex")),
    sep = "\n")
cat("\n\\end{center}\n")
```

*Note on the MO boundary.* The Missouri alpha hits the
$\pm 20$ Newton bound ($\hat\alpha_{\text{MO}} = 20.0$, SE $9.46$) because the
group's empirical Exit-share is essentially zero in the observed window;
the profile FOC has no interior solution and the solver correctly pins
the value at the bound with an inflated SE that honestly reports the
local non-identification. This is an improvement over the M-only joint
spec where the same data pathology surfaced as a spuriously-tight
$\hat\alpha_{\text{MO}} = 8.18$ (SE $0.77$).


# Model Comparison {#sec-comparison}

@tbl-comparison reports log-likelihood, information criteria, and nested
likelihood-ratio tests across the four specifications. All four are fit
on the same observed sample, so the comparison is direct.

```{r tbl-comparison, results="asis"}
#| label: tbl-comparison
#| tbl-cap: "Four-way model comparison. AIC $= -2\\log L + 2k$; BIC $= -2\\log L + k\\log N$. Likelihood-ratio tests reported: 8p vs 4p ($\\Delta k = 4$); 8p+FE M-only vs 8p ($\\Delta k = 17$); 8p+stayFE profile vs 8p ($\\Delta k = 17$). All three LR statistics are far in the tail of their reference $\\chi^2$ distributions, with $p$-values numerically zero. The two FE specifications are non-nested to each other (same effective $k = 25$, different $\\alpha$-placement), so they are not LR-comparable; AIC/BIC favor the M-only joint placement by $\\approx 1{,}500$ units."
cat("\\begin{center}\n")
cat(readLines(here("Output", "Tables", "04l_Model_Comparison_4way.tex")),
    sep = "\n")
cat("\n\\end{center}\n")
```

All three nested restrictions are rejected at any conventional level.
AIC and BIC agree on the ranking
4p $\succ$ 8p $\succ$ 8p+stayFE (profile) $\succ$ 8p+FE (M-only, joint) —
the joint-M-only fit is best in absolute likelihood terms.
However, the two FE specifications are *non-nested* to each other:
they differ in where $\alpha_g$ enters the flow utility, not in
parameter count. The profile-stayFE specification has the cleaner
economic interpretation ($\alpha_g$ shifts state-specific stay-vs-exit
propensity) at a cost of $\approx 750$ log-likelihood units relative
to the M-only joint fit.

## Aggregate fit-metric comparison {#sec-comparison-fit-metrics}

@tbl-fit-metrics-summary collapses the per-(wall, regime, action)
weighted-RMSE numbers from each model into a per-action summary
weighted by total cell sample size. Lower is better in every column.

```{r tbl-fit-metrics-summary}
#| label: tbl-fit-metrics-summary
#| tbl-cap: "Aggregate goodness-of-fit comparison across the four specifications. Each cell reports the cell-sample-size-weighted RMSE of the model-vs-empirical residual, computed as $\\sqrt{\\sum_c n_c \\cdot \\text{res}_c^2 / \\sum_c n_c}$ across the four $(\\text{wall} \\times \\text{regime})$ subgroups for the given action. Overall column collapses across all 12 wall-regime-action groups. Lower is better."
.summarize_fit <- function(path, label) {
  dt <- fread(path)
  agg <- dt[, .(
    weighted_RMSE = sqrt(sum(weighted_RMSE^2 * total_n) / sum(total_n))
  ), by = action]
  overall <- dt[, .(
    action = "Overall",
    weighted_RMSE = sqrt(sum(weighted_RMSE^2 * total_n) / sum(total_n))
  )]
  out <- rbind(agg, overall)
  out[, Model := label]
  setcolorder(out, c("Model", "action", "weighted_RMSE"))
  out
}
fit_metrics <- rbindlist(list(
  .summarize_fit(here("Output", "Tables", "04g_Fit_Quality_byWallRegimeAction.csv"),
                 "4-parameter"),
  .summarize_fit(here("Output", "Tables", "04h_8param_FitQuality_byWallRegimeAction.csv"),
                 "8-parameter"),
  .summarize_fit(here("Output", "Tables", "04k_FitQuality_8paramFE_byWallRegimeAction.csv"),
                 "8p+FE (M-only, joint)"),
  .summarize_fit(here("Output", "Tables", "04l_FitQuality_8paramFE_profile_byWallRegimeAction.csv"),
                 "8p+stayFE (profile)")
))
fit_metrics_wide <- dcast(fit_metrics, Model ~ action, value.var = "weighted_RMSE")
fit_metrics_wide <- fit_metrics_wide[, .(Model, Maintain, Exit, Replace, Overall)]
fit_metrics_wide[, Model := factor(Model, levels = c(
  "4-parameter","8-parameter","8p+FE (M-only, joint)","8p+stayFE (profile)"))]
setorder(fit_metrics_wide, Model)

kable(fit_metrics_wide, format = "latex", booktabs = TRUE, digits = 5,
      align = c("l","r","r","r","r")) |>
  kable_styling(latex_options = c("scale_down"), font_size = 9)
```

Takeaway: the 8p specifications all reduce overall weighted RMSE by
roughly half relative to 4p; among the 8p variants, the M-only-joint
spec achieves the lowest overall RMSE, with the stayFE-profile spec a
close second. The 4p model's poor fit is concentrated in *Exit* and
*Maintain* (where the wall/regime split matters most), while *Replace*
fit is already comparable across the 8p variants.


# Goodness of Fit: Model-Implied vs. Empirical Cell Shares {#sec-fit}

This section reports the model-implied conditional choice probability
$\hat P(\text{action} \mid \text{state})$ at the converged $\hat\theta$
alongside the empirical action share in each $(\text{wall} \times \text{regime}
\times \text{age bin})$ cell. Empirical shares are computed by direct cell
aggregation on the observed panel. Model-implied shares are read from the
NPL-converged $\hat P$ matrix saved in each fit object. For the 8p+FE
specification, $\hat P$ is the alpha-augmented, $g$-marginalized measurement
probability (the same object the NPL outer loop uses for its CCP update).

Each model has two figures (one per wall type), each with three facets
(Maintain / Exit / Replace) and two colored lines (FF / RB regime). All six
figures are produced by a single shared plotting routine so visual
comparability across specifications is structural.

## 4-parameter fit {#sec-fit-4p}

```{r fig-fit-4p-sw}
#| label: fig-fit-4p-sw
#| fig-cap: "4-parameter fit, Single-Walled tanks. Lines are model-implied $\\hat P(\\text{action} \\mid \\text{state})$; open circles are empirical cell shares with area proportional to cell sample size."
#| fig-width: 7
#| fig-height: 2.8
knitr::include_graphics(here("Output", "Figures", "04k_Fit_4param_SW.png"))
```

```{r fig-fit-4p-dw}
#| label: fig-fit-4p-dw
#| fig-cap: "4-parameter fit, Double-Walled tanks. Same construction as @fig-fit-4p-sw."
#| fig-width: 7
#| fig-height: 2.8
knitr::include_graphics(here("Output", "Figures", "04k_Fit_4param_DW.png"))
```

```{r tbl-fitq-4p}
#| label: tbl-fitq-4p
#| tbl-cap: "4-parameter weighted-RMSE summary of model-vs-empirical residuals by wall, regime, and action. Residual $=$ empirical $-$ model share; weights are cell sample size."
fq4 <- fread(here("Output", "Tables", "04g_Fit_Quality_byWallRegimeAction.csv"))
kable(fq4, format = "latex", booktabs = TRUE, digits = 5,
      align = c("l","l","l", rep("r", ncol(fq4) - 3L))) |>
  kable_styling(latex_options = c("scale_down"), font_size = 9)
```

## 8-parameter fit {#sec-fit-8p}

```{r fig-fit-8p-sw}
#| label: fig-fit-8p-sw
#| fig-cap: "8-parameter fit, Single-Walled tanks. Same construction as @fig-fit-4p-sw."
#| fig-width: 7
#| fig-height: 2.8
knitr::include_graphics(here("Output", "Figures", "04k_Fit_8param_SW.png"))
```

```{r fig-fit-8p-dw}
#| label: fig-fit-8p-dw
#| fig-cap: "8-parameter fit, Double-Walled tanks. Same construction as @fig-fit-4p-sw."
#| fig-width: 7
#| fig-height: 2.8
knitr::include_graphics(here("Output", "Figures", "04k_Fit_8param_DW.png"))
```

```{r tbl-fitq-8p}
#| label: tbl-fitq-8p
#| tbl-cap: "8-parameter weighted-RMSE summary by wall, regime, and action. Same construction as @tbl-fitq-4p."
fq8 <- fread(here("Output", "Tables", "04h_8param_FitQuality_byWallRegimeAction.csv"))
kable(fq8, format = "latex", booktabs = TRUE, digits = 5,
      align = c("l","l","l", rep("r", ncol(fq8) - 3L))) |>
  kable_styling(latex_options = c("scale_down"), font_size = 9)
```

## 8-parameter + FE (M-only, joint) fit {#sec-fit-8pfe}

```{r fig-fit-8pfe-sw}
#| label: fig-fit-8pfe-sw
#| fig-cap: "8-parameter + FE fit, Single-Walled tanks. Model-implied probabilities marginalize the per-$g$ choice probability over the empirical state composition $w_g(s)$. Same plot construction as @fig-fit-4p-sw."
#| fig-width: 7
#| fig-height: 2.8
knitr::include_graphics(here("Output", "Figures", "04k_Fit_8paramFE_SW.png"))
```

```{r fig-fit-8pfe-dw}
#| label: fig-fit-8pfe-dw
#| fig-cap: "8-parameter + FE fit, Double-Walled tanks. Same construction as @fig-fit-8pfe-sw."
#| fig-width: 7
#| fig-height: 2.8
knitr::include_graphics(here("Output", "Figures", "04k_Fit_8paramFE_DW.png"))
```

```{r tbl-fitq-8pfe}
#| label: tbl-fitq-8pfe
#| tbl-cap: "8-parameter + FE (M-only, joint) weighted-RMSE summary by wall, regime, and action. Same construction as @tbl-fitq-4p."
fqfe <- fread(here("Output", "Tables", "04k_FitQuality_8paramFE_byWallRegimeAction.csv"))
kable(fqfe, format = "latex", booktabs = TRUE, digits = 5,
      align = c("l","l","l", rep("r", ncol(fqfe) - 3L))) |>
  kable_styling(latex_options = c("scale_down"), font_size = 9)
```

## 8-parameter + stayFE (profile) fit {#sec-fit-8pfeProfile}

```{r fig-fit-8pfeProfile-sw}
#| label: fig-fit-8pfeProfile-sw
#| fig-cap: "8-parameter + stayFE (profile) fit, Single-Walled tanks. Same construction as @fig-fit-4p-sw. Model-implied probabilities marginalize the per-$g$ choice probability over the empirical state composition $w_g(s)$, with $\\alpha_g$ entering both Maintain and Replace utilities (profiled out via 1-D FOC)."
#| fig-width: 7
#| fig-height: 2.8
knitr::include_graphics(here("Output", "Figures", "04l_Fit_8paramFE_profile_SW.png"))
```

```{r fig-fit-8pfeProfile-dw}
#| label: fig-fit-8pfeProfile-dw
#| fig-cap: "8-parameter + stayFE (profile) fit, Double-Walled tanks. Same construction as @fig-fit-8pfeProfile-sw."
#| fig-width: 7
#| fig-height: 2.8
knitr::include_graphics(here("Output", "Figures", "04l_Fit_8paramFE_profile_DW.png"))
```

```{r tbl-fitq-8pfeProfile}
#| label: tbl-fitq-8pfeProfile
#| tbl-cap: "8-parameter + stayFE (profile) weighted-RMSE summary by wall, regime, and action. Same construction as @tbl-fitq-4p."
fqfeProfile <- fread(here("Output", "Tables", "04l_FitQuality_8paramFE_profile_byWallRegimeAction.csv"))
kable(fqfeProfile, format = "latex", booktabs = TRUE, digits = 5,
      align = c("l","l","l", rep("r", ncol(fqfeProfile) - 3L))) |>
  kable_styling(latex_options = c("scale_down"), font_size = 9)
```


# Appendix: Per-cell fit numbers {#sec-appendix}

The following three tables report the full 32-row cell-level fit numbers
for each specification. Columns: `Reg` (regime: FF/RB), `Wall` (SW/DW),
`Age` (5-year bin), `N` (cell sample size), and for each of the three
actions $a \in \{M, E, R\}$, `mod_a` $= \hat P_a(s)$ (model-implied),
`emp_a` (empirical share), and `res_a` $=$ `emp_a` $-$ `mod_a` (residual).

```{r appendix-prep, include=FALSE}
.prep_appendix <- function(path) {
  dt <- fread(path)
  dt <- dt[, .(regime, wall, age_label, n_cell,
               model_maintain, emp_maintain, res_maintain,
               model_exit,     emp_exit,     res_exit,
               model_replace,  emp_replace,  res_replace)]
  setnames(dt,
    c("regime","wall","age_label","n_cell",
      "model_maintain","emp_maintain","res_maintain",
      "model_exit",    "emp_exit",    "res_exit",
      "model_replace", "emp_replace", "res_replace"),
    c("Reg","Wall","Age","N",
      "mod_M","emp_M","res_M",
      "mod_E","emp_E","res_E",
      "mod_R","emp_R","res_R"))
  dt
}
.render_appendix <- function(dt) {
  kable(dt, format = "latex", booktabs = TRUE, longtable = TRUE,
        digits = 4,
        align = c("l","l","l","r", rep("r", 9))) |>
    kable_styling(font_size = 8)
}
```

## 4-parameter {#sec-appendix-4p}

```{r tbl-appendix-4p}
#| label: tbl-appendix-4p
#| tbl-cap: "4-parameter per-cell fit: model-implied probability, empirical share, and residual by state cell."
.render_appendix(.prep_appendix(here("Output", "Tables", "04g_Per_Cell_Fit_Numbers_Wide.csv")))
```

## 8-parameter {#sec-appendix-8p}

```{r tbl-appendix-8p}
#| label: tbl-appendix-8p
#| tbl-cap: "8-parameter per-cell fit. Same construction as @tbl-appendix-4p."
.render_appendix(.prep_appendix(here("Output", "Tables", "04h_8param_PerCell_Fit_Wide.csv")))
```

## 8-parameter + FE (M-only, joint) {#sec-appendix-8pfe}

```{r tbl-appendix-8pfe}
#| label: tbl-appendix-8pfe
#| tbl-cap: "8-parameter + FE (M-only, joint) per-cell fit. Same construction as @tbl-appendix-4p."
.render_appendix(.prep_appendix(here("Output", "Tables", "04k_PerCell_Fit_8paramFE_Wide.csv")))
```

## 8-parameter + stayFE (profile) {#sec-appendix-8pfeProfile}

```{r tbl-appendix-8pfeProfile}
#| label: tbl-appendix-8pfeProfile
#| tbl-cap: "8-parameter + stayFE (profile) per-cell fit. Same construction as @tbl-appendix-4p."
.render_appendix(.prep_appendix(here("Output", "Tables", "04l_PerCell_Fit_8paramFE_profile_Wide.csv")))
```
